content.text:
Moving `useMemo` before the early return to satisfy the rules of hooks.

[REDACTED]

Here is what changed:

### Shared module `features/requests/rulesetDocuments.ts`
- Moved `buildRulesetDocumentsForInquiries` (and its helpers) out of the wizard so both the wizard and the detail panel use the same logic.
- `CertificationRequestInquiry` is the same type as `CertificationRequestDraft` in your domain, so **`request.inquiries`** drives the same PTV / ATG / EPD / ruleset matrix / checklist list as the review step.

### `CertificationRequestWizard.tsx`
- Imports `buildRulesetDocumentsForInquiries` from `../requests/rulesetDocuments` and drops the duplicated inline implementation.

### `request_detail_panel.tsx`
- Renders **`DownloadableDocumentsList`** after “Vervolgacties” and before “Levenscyclus”, with copy aimed at an existing pakket (“Documenten gekoppeld aan de … aanvragen in dit pakket”).
- `useMemo` runs **before** the `!request` return so hooks stay unconditional.

Typecheck for `apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding` passes, and the conditional-hooks lint is resolved.

**Note:** In the wizard, the list still reflects only **checked** drafts (`includedReviewInquiries`); in the detail panel it reflects **all** inquiries stored on the package (what was actually submitted / persisted). If you later need the detail view to match “only included” for drafts still in progress, you’d need that inclusion state on `CertificationRequestPackage`; for submitted packages the two should match.

[REDACTED]

So scroll order is already: actions → lifecycle → inquiries → documents. No reordering is required for that layout.

If you instead want that block **stuck to the lower edge of the slide-over** while the rest scrolls separately (a fixed “footer” strip), say so and we can adjust the layout around `CoverView` / scroll for that pattern.

input.contents:
export { PanelSection } from "./PanelSection";
export type { PanelSectionProps } from "./PanelSection";

# PanelSection

Scaffolded with **`scaffold workspace-ui add-component PanelSection --variant section`**, then adapted to a **detail-panel section** pattern (optional header, body slot only — no marketing CTAs).

## Purpose

- **`packages/ui-lib`**: shared **presentational** block for request detail panels, settings sidebars, and any `Card` that needs the same header + body spacing.
- **Optional `title` / `description`** — omit both for a headerless card (body only). Empty strings are treated as absent.
- **`contentClassName`** — pass layout utilities for the body (e.g. `flex flex-wrap gap-2` for action rows).

## Checklist

1. Keep **routing, mutations, and confirm flows** in apps; pass results or handlers into `children` only where the child is already a dumb component.
2. **Stories** in `PanelSection.stories.tsx` cover header combinations and follow-up actions.
3. **Exports**: `index.ts` and root `packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts` (scaffold updates the root export when the component is added).

content.text:
Here’s what was done to **use the scaffold CLI** and still match your detail-panel design.

### 1. Scaffold (official command)

From `packages/ui-lib`:

```bash
scaffold workspace-ui add-component PanelSection --variant section
```

That created the usual **ui-lib** layout: `PanelSection.tsx`, `index.ts`, `PanelSection.stories.tsx`, `COMPONENT.md`, and it **registered the export** in `packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts`.

The **`section`** template is a marketing-style block (badge, separator, dual CTAs). That doesn’t match a request detail panel, so the generated component was **replaced** with your **optional title / optional description / `children` / `contentClassName`** API, and `COMPONENT.md` was updated to describe that (including a note that it was scaffolded then adapted).

### 2. Public API (unchanged from your intent)

- Optional **`title`** and **`description`** (`ReactNode`; empty strings treated as absent).
- Required **`children`** as the main body.
- **`className`** on the `Card`, **`contentClassName`** on `CardContent` (e.g. `flex flex-wrap gap-2` for actions).

### 3. Stories (`PanelSection.stories.tsx`)

Stories now cover: **title + description**, **title only**, **description only**, **headerless**, **follow-up actions** (same pattern as “Vervolgacties”), **long description in a narrow column**, **rich `ReactNode` title**.

### 4. App wiring

`request_detail_panel.tsx` imports **`PanelSection`** from `@procertus-ui/ui-lib`, drops the local helper, and uses **`PanelSection`** for **Vervolgacties** (with `contentClassName`) plus the existing lifecycle / inquiries sections.

### 5. Extra fix

`DownloadableDocumentsList.stories.tsx` was adjusted so **`tsc -p packages/ui-lib`** passes (Default uses `args`; SingleRow is cast where the story doesn’t use the list component).

---

**Note:** For a **blank** marketing section, the CLI’s **`--variant section`** is the right scaffold hook; for **new** components that are only “card + optional header + body”, **`--variant composed`** or **`minimal`** may be closer next time—then you’d trim props instead of removing badge/footer CTAs.

[REDACTED]

</user_query>

# PanelSection Scaffolded with **`scaffold workspace-ui add-component PanelSection --variant section`**, then adapted for **detail panels** (no card chrome). ## Purpose - **`packages/ui-lib`**: presentational **section stack** — optional **`h2` title**, optional **description** (`

`, muted), then **`children`**. - **Spacing:** `gap-1` between title and description; **`gap-section`** between the intro block and the body (design tokens). - **Optional `title` / `description`** — omit both for a body-only block. Empty strings are treated as absent. - **`contentClassName`** — layout on the body wrapper (e.g. `flex flex-wrap gap-2` for action rows). ## Checklist 1. Keep **routing, mutations, and confirm flows** in apps; `PanelSection` stays presentational. 2. **Stories** in `PanelSection.stories.tsx` cover header combinations and follow-up actions. 3. **Exports**: `index.ts` and root `packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts`.

content.text:
Here’s what was updated:

### `DownloadableDocumentListItem` (`DownloadableDocumentsList.tsx`)
- Renders a Shadcn **`Item`** with `role="listitem"`, **`variant="outline"`**, **`size="sm"`**.
- **`ItemMedia`** (`variant="icon"`) — file icon.
- **`ItemContent`** — **`ItemTitle`** wraps an underlined **`<a download>`** for the title; **`ItemDescription`** holds optional description + format hint (`<span>` when both exist).
- **`ItemActions`** — same icon **download** control as before; **`aria-label`** stays on that control only (title link uses visible text).

### `DownloadableDocumentsList`
- Replaced the inner **`<ul>`** with **`ItemGroup`** (`className="w-full"`) and still maps **`DownloadableDocumentListItem`** (same `role="list"` / `listitem` pairing as `item.tsx`).

### `request_detail_panel.tsx`
- Replaced the **`<ul>`** around ruleset docs with **`ItemGroup`** from `@procertus-ui/ui`.

### Stories & docs
- **`SingleRow`** story uses **`ItemGroup`** instead of a raw `<ul>`.
- **`COMPONENT.md`** notes **`Item` / `ItemGroup`** usage.

`@procertus-ui/ui` already exports **`Item`**, **`ItemGroup`**, etc. via `components/ui`.

content.text:
Here’s what changed in `CertificationRequestCard.tsx`:

### Imports
Extended the `@procertus-ui/ui` import with **`Item`**, **`ItemActions`**, **`ItemContent`**, **`ItemDescription`**, **`ItemGroup`**, and **`ItemTitle`**.

### `InquirySummary`
- Replaced the **`<ul>` / `<li>`** block with **`ItemGroup`** (`className="w-full gap-2"` so spacing stays like the old `grid gap-2`).
- Each inquiry is an **`Item`** with **`role="listitem"`**, **`variant="outline"`**, **`size="sm"`**, and **`className="min-w-0 border-border/70 bg-muted/25 shadow-proc-xs"`** so it stays close to the previous bordered, muted, shadowed look.
- **Copy / layout:** **`ItemTitle`** (`line-clamp-2 font-semibold`) for **`inquiry.label`**, **`ItemDescription`** (`line-clamp-2 text-xs`) for **`inquiry.productLabel ?? "Niet-productgebonden"`**, **`ItemActions`** for the outline **`Badge`**s with the same flex-wrap / gap as before.

`ItemGroup` already sets **`role="list"`** in `item.tsx`, so that wasn’t duplicated on the wrapper.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string: Presentational list of document rows. **`DownloadableDocumentListItem`** uses **`Item asChild`** so the whole row is one **`<a download>`** (hover/focus on the anchor per [shadcn Item / Link](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/item#link)): **`ItemMedia`** (file icon), **`ItemContent`** (title + description), **`ItemActions`** (decorative download icon only, `aria-hidden`).
input.old_string: Presentational list of document rows with a download action. **`DownloadableDocumentListItem`** composes Shadcn **`Item`** (`ItemMedia`, `ItemContent`, `ItemTitle`, `ItemDescription`, `ItemActions`). **`DownloadableDocumentsList`** wraps rows in **`ItemGroup`** inside a **`Card`**; use **`ItemGroup`** + **`DownloadableDocumentListItem`** when you supply your own section chrome (e.g. `PanelSection`).

content.text:
Here’s what changed in `DownloadableDocumentListItem`:

### `Item asChild` + single download link
- **`Item asChild`** wraps one **`<a href={href} download rel="noopener noreferrer">`**, following the [shadcn Item “Link” pattern](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/item#) so **hover/focus styles apply to the anchor**.
- **`aria-label`** on that anchor comes from **`downloadAriaLabel`** or **`Download ${title}`** (whole row is the control).

### Layout
- **`ItemMedia`** — file icon (unchanged, `aria-hidden`).
- **`ItemContent`** — **`ItemTitle`** is plain **title** text with underline utilities + **`group-hover/item:`** so the title still reads as the main affordance.
- **`ItemDescription`** — unchanged (description + optional `formatHint` in a `span`).
- **`ItemActions`** — **`Download01Icon` only** (no **`Button`**, no nested link). Marked **`aria-hidden`** so the row **`aria-label`** isn’t doubled.

### Cleanup
- Dropped the **`Button`** import from this file.
- **`COMPONENT.md`** updated to describe the full-row link and decorative download icon.

`DownloadableDocumentsList` / **`ItemGroup`** usage is unchanged.

[REDACTED]

input.command:
cd /Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment && git commit -m "feat(ui-lib,extranet): add PanelSection, Item rows, and shared ruleset docs" -m "Extract buildRulesetDocumentsForInquiries into apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding so the certification wizard and request detail panel share the same mock document metadata.

Add PanelSection in ui-lib (optional title and description, section spacing without card chrome) with Storybook coverage. The request detail panel uses PanelSection for follow-up actions, lifecycle, inquiries, and rules documentation; inquiry and rules lists use ItemGroup and Shadcn Item primitives.

DownloadableDocumentListItem uses Item asChild so the full row is one download anchor, with a decorative download icon in ItemActions instead of a separate button. DownloadableDocumentsList wraps rows in ItemGroup; stories were adjusted.

CertificationRequestCard inquiry previews now use ItemGroup and Item for the same item pattern.

Left .scaffold/metrics/snapshots.jsonl unstaged as scaffold telemetry rather than product source."
input.description: Create git commit with conventional message and body

content.text:
Created commit **`8f1b6ed`** on `main` following [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) and `skills/git-commits/SKILL.md`:

**Subject:** `feat(ui-lib,extranet): add PanelSection, Item rows, and shared ruleset docs`

**Included (12 files):** shared `rulesetDocuments.ts`, wizard + detail panel updates, `PanelSection` in ui-lib (with stories + `COMPONENT.md`), `DownloadableDocumentsList` / stories / `COMPONENT.md`, root `ui-lib` exports, and `CertificationRequestCard` Item layout.

**Not committed:** `.scaffold/metrics/snapshots.jsonl` — one new scaffold metrics line; treated as tooling telemetry, not product code (per the skill’s “avoid generated noise” guidance). It is still modified locally; run `git restore .scaffold/metrics/snapshots.jsonl` if you want a clean tree, or commit it separately if your team tracks those snapshots.

Your branch is **ahead of `origin/main` by 1 commit**; push when ready.
